notes: Lenalidomide is a potent inhibitor of TNF-alpha production, a core IMiD property. TNF role in keloids is context-dependent (pro-proliferative via NF-kB but also induces MMPs), so net effect is unclear.
Lenalidomide reduces VEGF expression in myeloma cell microvesicles, contributing to anti-angiogenic activity. Indirect effect via cereblon-mediated pathways.
Lenalidomide has antiangiogenic effects on endothelial cells and suppresses VEGF receptor signaling. Related IMiDs shown to affect KDR/VEGFR2 expression on endothelial progenitor cells.
Lenalidomide inhibits beta-catenin associations with cadherin-5 and CD31 in endothelial cells. Systems biology analysis identifies CTNNB1 as central IMiD-targeted transcription factor.
Indirect effect — rituximab depletes CD20+ B cells, which are major IL-6 producers. RA clinical studies demonstrate significant serum IL-6 reduction following rituximab treatment.
No direct or well-documented indirect effect on JAK2. Rituximab targets CD20 on B cells; any JAK2 modulation would be a distant downstream consequence of reduced cytokine milieu.
Indirect — B cells produce TGF-β1; depletion via rituximab may reduce TGF-β1 levels. Limited evidence from systemic sclerosis studies suggests reduced TGF-β signaling post-rituximab.
Imatinib does not directly inhibit EGFR. Its kinase targets are ABL, PDGFR-β, and c-KIT — a distinct kinase family from EGFR/ErbB. No published evidence of meaningful EGFR modulation.

Indirect effect — imatinib modulates NF-kB/JAK2/STAT3 pathway in colitis model. However, CML studies show JAK2 requires separate targeting to overcome imatinib resistance, implying imatinib does not directly inhibit JAK2 kinase activity.

Imatinib blocks non-canonical TGF-β signaling via c-Abl inhibition, reducing SMAD3-mediated fibrogenesis. Well-demonstrated in renal and pulmonary fibrosis models. Mechanism is indirect (c-Abl → TGF-β → SMAD3) but robust.

Imatinib mediates antineoplastic effects via Wnt/β-catenin in anaplastic thyroid cancer. Wnt/β-catenin is targetable in GIST context. Relevance to keloid fibroblasts is uncertain — evidence is cancer-specific.
COX-2-independent inhibition of STAT3 phosphorylation. Celecoxib directly inhibits STAT3 activation at concentrations achievable in vivo, demonstrated in glioblastoma and HNSCC models.
Celecoxib suppresses PI3K/AKT signaling in multiple cancer models. Some evidence suggests COX-2-independent mechanism via direct PDK1 inhibition upstream of AKT.
COX-2 inhibition reduces PGE2-mediated VEGF induction. Celecoxib decreases VEGF expression in tumor and endothelial cells. Well-documented anti-angiogenic property of COX-2 inhibitors.
Celecoxib downregulates Wnt/β-catenin signaling in colorectal cancer models. Reduces nuclear β-catenin accumulation and TCF/LEF transcriptional activity, partially independent of COX-2 inhibition.

IFN-α has anti-proliferative effects that include downregulation of EGFR expression and signaling. Evidence primarily from epidermoid carcinoma and other epithelial models; fibroblast-specific data is limited.

IFN-α suppresses pro-inflammatory cytokine production including IL-6 in fibrotic contexts. Consistent with clinical anti-fibrotic use in keloids, though the effect is indirect via broad immunomodulation.

Type I interferons signal through IFNAR → JAK-STAT pathway. While canonical signaling uses JAK1/TYK2, JAK2 associates with the IFNAR complex and is activated during type I IFN signaling. Activating a pro-keloid target is unfavorable.

GR activation interferes with SMAD3-dependent transcription via direct GR-SMAD3 protein interaction and induction of inhibitory SMAD7. Demonstrated in lung fibroblasts — reduces TGF-β1-induced SMAD3 phosphorylation.
Potent VEGF suppressor — clinically exploited in intravitreal implants (Ozurdex). GR activation suppresses VEGF transcription via NF-kB and AP-1 inhibition and reduces HIF-1a-mediated VEGF induction.
Corticosteroids downregulate VEGFR2/KDR expression on endothelial cells. Contributes to anti-angiogenic effects beyond VEGF suppression. Demonstrated in dermal microvascular endothelial cells.

GR activation can induce GSK-3B activity promoting beta-catenin degradation and interact with TCF/LEF to suppress Wnt target genes. Evidence is mixed across cell types — low confidence for keloid fibroblasts specifically.

Thalidomide is the original TNF-alpha inhibitor — selectively inhibits TNF-alpha production by accelerating mRNA degradation (Sampaio et al. 1991). High confidence based on extensive clinical and preclinical validation. TNF role in keloids is context-dependent.

Thalidomide has potent anti-angiogenic activity including suppression of VEGF secretion. Originally discovered as anti-angiogenic in a rabbit corneal micropocket assay. Clinically exploited in multiple myeloma where VEGF drives tumor angiogenesis.

IMiDs modulate Wnt/beta-catenin signaling via cereblon-mediated substrate degradation. Thalidomide disrupts beta-catenin associations with cadherin-5 and CD31, similar to lenalidomide. Cereblon interaction is the molecular basis for all IMiD activities.

Canonical mTOR inhibitor. FKBP12-sirolimus complex directly inhibits mTORC1 kinase activity, blocking S6K1 and 4E-BP1 phosphorylation. Well-validated in fibrosis models — reduces fibroblast proliferation and collagen synthesis.

Complex: mTOR is downstream of PI3K, but mTORC1 inhibition relieves S6K-mediated negative feedback on IRS-1, potentially activating PI3K/AKT (O'Reilly 2006). Net PI3K pathway activity depends on cell context. In fibrosis models, rapamycin generally suppresses proliferation despite feedback.
Indirect — mTOR inhibition reduces cap-dependent translation, which can lower EGFR protein levels. Some evidence that rapamycin sensitizes cells to EGFR inhibitors. No direct EGFR kinase inhibition.

Indirect — mTOR inhibition reduces JAK-STAT signaling output in myeloproliferative models. Rapamycin suppresses JAK2-V617F-driven proliferation in synergy with JAK2 inhibitors. No direct JAK2 kinase inhibition.

Aspirin inhibits PI3K/AKT signaling via COX-independent mechanisms. Disrupts K-Ras–p110α interaction in leiomyoma cells and shows enhanced efficacy in PIK3CA-mutant colorectal cancer via PI3K/Akt/Raptor pathway suppression.

Aspirin suppresses TGF-β1–Smad2/Smad3 pathway, reducing endometrial fibrosis markers. Aspirin-triggered resolvin D1 further inhibits TGF-β1-induced EMT via mTOR/PKM2 pathway. Directly relevant to anti-fibrotic action in keloids.

No published evidence of aspirin modulating CYP24A1 expression or enzymatic activity. Only PubMed association is a pharmacogenomic SNP (rs4809957) linked to aspirin-induced GI bleeding risk — not a mechanistic drug-gene interaction.
